text: I’ll inspect the fixture app’s manifests, test setup, and available bomly tooling first, then I’ll patch only the dependency files and the required `FIXES.md`.

The repo is a Maven multi-module fixture under `fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent`; root `make test` runs a skipped-test Maven install, so dependency remediation needs to preserve that reactor build. I’m loading the bomly tool surface now to get the vulnerability inventory instead of relying on manifest guessing.

The compact scan found 21 vulnerable packages and its response was capped, so I’m switching to the bomly CLI JSON output to avoid missing anything. After that I’ll explain the exact packages that need either property bumps or dependencyManagement overrides.

The vulnerable set is mostly transitives pulled through the parent reactor: protobuf from MySQL, Bouncy Castle and jose4j from the Box module, Netty/Nimbus/OAuth/Jackson from Qpid and Google provisioning paths. I’m checking the full JSON now because the first response hid several packages behind truncation.
That CLI invocation used the wrong argument form; I’m checking the command syntax and will rerun it with the path flag.

The parent POM already centralizes versions, including the dependencyManagement section where transitive pins belong. I found existing `bcprov-jdk18on`/`bcpkix-jdk18on` management, but the vulnerable Box path is still using the older `jdk15on` artifacts, so those need explicit management too.

The JSON file was written despite the policy-exit code. I’m extracting only vulnerability findings, not license policy warnings, because the request is specifically to remediate known-vulnerable dependencies.
`jq` is not installed here, so I’m using the runtime that’s available to summarize the scan data. This is read-only parsing, not a repo edit.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const f=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-scan.json'); const by={}; for(const x of f.findings.filter(x=>x.kind==='vulnerability')){const k=x.package.name+'@'+x.package.version; (by[k]??=[]).push(x.id)} console.log(Object.entries(by).sort().map(([k,v])=>k+' '+v.length).join('\\n'))\""

text: Some findings do not include a `Fix available` line in the raw JSON, so I’m drilling into those packages with bomly before choosing versions. That matters for your “no fixed version exists” requirement.

Bomly classifies four packages as `no-fix-upstream`: `commons-httpclient`, `commons-lang`, `hibernate-core`, and `ion-java`. I won’t invent fixes for those; I’ll document them explicitly. For the remaining packages, I’m going to use parent-level version updates and dependencyManagement pins so the reactor keeps its current module structure.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "grep -R \"axis2.version\\|mime4j\\|neethi\\|commons.compress\\|guava\\|protobuf\\|nimbus-jose\\|google-oauth-client\\|netty\" -n fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent/pom.xml fixtures/bigapp/grouper-ws/pom.xml fixtures/bigapp/grouper-ws/*/pom.xml fixtures/bigapp/grouper-misc/*/pom.xml fixtures/bigapp/grouper/pom.xml | head -220"

text: I found one more nuance: `jackson-databind` has three fixable advisories resolved by `2.18.8`, but one separate advisory is marked `no-fix-upstream`. I’ll still bump to `2.18.8` to close the fixable ones and document the remaining one as unresolved upstream.

The Bouncy Castle recommendation pointed at versions that do not exist for the old `jdk15on` coordinates. I’m checking the highest available `jdk15on` line before deciding whether this is a version bump, an artifact replacement, or an explicit “no fixed version on this coordinate” entry.

For Bouncy Castle, the viable remediation is to stop resolving the vulnerable `jdk15on` artifacts from `box-java-sdk` and use the maintained `jdk15to18` artifacts at `1.84`; the package namespace is the same Java API surface but the Maven coordinates are no longer the vulnerable discontinued line.

I verified `jackson-databind`/core `2.18.9` exists, which should cover the advisory whose affected range stops before `2.18.9` even though the scanner labeled it no-fix. I’m going to prefer the higher available patch version and let the follow-up scan confirm.

Maven confirms there is no published `commons-lang:2.7`, `commons-httpclient:3.2`, or `hibernate-core:5.6.16.Final`; those scanner no-fix classifications hold for the current artifact lines. The Ion advisory also has no `1.10.5` artifact under the current coordinate, so I’m leaving it documented unless a later available version proves usable in the scan.

I’m editing the parent dependency management plus two module POMs now: parent for centralized pins, `grouper-box` for the direct jose4j version and Bouncy Castle artifact replacement, and WS POMs where `axis2-transport-http` had explicit vulnerable versions.
The parent pins are in place. I’m applying the smaller module-level edits now for direct dependencies that dependencyManagement cannot override by itself.
Next I’m doing a fast Maven validation of the updated dependency graph before the full root build. This should catch missing artifacts or incompatible explicit versions early.
The Maven validate pass succeeded across the 13-module reactor. I’m rescanning with bomly now to see exactly which advisories closed and which no-fix items remain.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const f=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-scan-after.json'); const by={}; for(const x of f.findings.filter(x=>x.kind==='vulnerability')){const k=x.package.name+'@'+x.package.version; (by[k]??=[]).push(x.id+' '+x.severity+' '+x.title)} console.log('packages',Object.keys(by).length); for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(by).sort()) console.log(k+'\\n  '+[...new Set(v)].sort().join('\\n  '));\""

text: The rescan removed 16 of the 21 vulnerable packages. `commons-compress` is still pinned explicitly in `grouper-installer`, so I’m correcting that direct version; the other four remaining packages are the no-fix-upstream lines already identified.
`commons-compress` was a simple explicit-version miss, not a scanner limitation. I’m changing it to the parent property so it stays aligned with the managed fix.
I’m running the vulnerability scan again after the explicit `commons-compress` fix. If the remaining set is only no-fix-upstream packages, I’ll document them and then run the full `make test`.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const f=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-scan-after2.json'); const by={}; for(const x of f.findings.filter(x=>x.kind==='vulnerability')){const k=x.package.name+'@'+x.package.version; (by[k]??=[]).push(x.id+' '+x.severity+' '+x.title)} console.log('packages',Object.keys(by).length); for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(by).sort()) console.log(k+'\\n  '+[...new Set(v)].sort().join('\\n  '));\""

text: The second rescan now shows only four vulnerable packages, all already confirmed as no-fix-upstream/no published patched version on their current coordinates. I’m writing `FIXES.md` with one entry for every package from the original 21-package inventory, including those four unresolved cases.
`FIXES.md` is now populated with all 21 original vulnerable packages and their actions. I’m running the required root `make test` now.
The full build is still running; it has made it through the WS module and is compiling the installer. The output is very noisy with pre-existing checkstyle warnings, but no failure yet.
`make test` completed successfully across the full reactor. I’m doing a final status check so I can separate intentional POM/FIXES changes from generated build artifacts.

The working tree has only the intended dependency files plus `FIXES.md`; the untracked `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `GEMINI.md` were already present before my edits. I’m grabbing the final diff summary now.

Implemented dependency remediation under `fixtures/` and added [FIXES.md](/tmp/bomly-study-4h2e6ppb/codex-mcp-bigapp-5-1783763298/repo/FIXES.md).

Changed the parent/module POMs to pin or bump the fixable vulnerable packages: Jackson, Guava, Google OAuth client, protobuf, Nimbus JOSE JWT, Netty, Axis2 transport, commons-compress, mime4j, Neethi, jose4j, and replaced old Bouncy Castle `jdk15on` transitives with maintained `jdk15to18` artifacts.

Confirmed with bomly that the original 21 vulnerable packages are down to 4 remaining packages with no version-only fix available:
`commons-httpclient:3.1`, `commons-lang:2.6`, `org.hibernate:hibernate-core:5.6.10.Final`, `software.amazon.ion:ion-java:1.0.2`.

Verification:
`make test` passed successfully.
